Barclays PLC
Peer Benchmarking Analysis
Data as of FY-12/2025  |  Market data as of February 2026
Peer group: JPMorgan, Bank of America, Citigroup, Wells Fargo, Goldman Sachs,
HSBC, BNP Paribas, Deutsche Bank, Crédit Agricole, Lloyds, American Express
Confidential
Barclays PLC — Peer Benchmarking Analysis
Overview
Barclays in Context: A Peer Snapshot
FY2025 — Barclays PLC vs. 11 global banking peers
Barclays ranks in the middle of the peer group across most key metrics, but the direction of travel is consistently upward. Operating income growth of 10.9% was near the top of the group. Pre-tax ROE has improved to 12.5%, and the cost/income ratio has dropped below 60% for the first time in the observed period.
12.5%
Pre-Tax ROE
Rank: 9th of 12
59.9%
Cost / Income
Rank: 5th of 11
10.9%
OI Growth YoY
Rank: 2nd of 12
48.4%
1-Year TRS
Rank: 3rd of 12
Pre-Tax ROE (FY2025)
Operating Income Growth (FY2025 YoY)
Cost / Income Ratio (FY2025) — lower is better
1-Year Total Return to Shareholders
Barclays is improving faster than most peers across multiple dimensions. The question is whether this momentum is structural or cyclical — and what it would take to move from mid-table to top quartile.
Confidential
Barclays PLC — Peer Benchmarking Analysis
Revenue
The Income Story: What's Driving Performance
Revenue composition and business segment contribution
Barclays operates with a near-even split between net interest income (49.8%) and non-interest income (50.2%) — a relatively unusual position among peers. Most banks lean heavily toward one or the other. Total operating income grew from £25.4bn to £29.1bn over the period, a 7.1% CAGR, driven by balanced growth across both revenue lines.
Operating Income Composition (GBP mn)
FY23
£25.4bn
FY24
£26.2bn
FY25
£29.1bn
Net Interest Income

Segment% of Op. IncomeEBT Margin FY23EBT Margin FY25Cost/Income FY23Cost/Income FY25Loan Loss Ratio FY25
Investment Bank45.2%29.0%35.3%70.1%62.3%2.3%
Barclays UK30.1%37.8%39.2%58.2%56.1%4.7%
US Consumer Bank12.7%5.3%14.0%50.7%44.7%41.3%
UK Corporate Bank7.1%49.8%47.0%51.5%51.2%1.8%
Private Bank & WM4.8%33.7%27.2%66.0%73.4%-0.6%
The Investment Bank — 45% of income — has driven significant margin expansion. How sustainable is this, and what does the segment mix tell us about where future growth comes from?
Confidential
Barclays PLC — Peer Benchmarking Analysis
Efficiency
The Efficiency Picture: Progress and Composition
Cost/income ratio trend and decomposition
Barclays' cost/income ratio dropped from 61.6% to 59.9% — breaking below 60% for the first time in the period. However, the composition tells an interesting story. Staff costs have fallen steadily from 39.5% to 36.5% of income, suggesting genuine workforce productivity gains. The non-staff cost line moved from 22.2% to 25.0% before partially correcting to 23.5% — it remains above where it started.
Barclays Cost Decomposition (% of Income)
22.2%
39.5%
FY23
61.6%

FY24

Staff Cost
Non-Staff Cost
Staff Cost / Income — Peer Comparison (FY2025)
Cost / Income Ratio — Full Peer Ranking (FY2025, lower is better)
The bank has made visible progress on workforce efficiency. The question the data raises is what's happening beneath the non-staff cost line — is this investment spend, structural cost, or something else?
Confidential
Barclays PLC — Peer Benchmarking Analysis
Credit Quality
Credit Quality: A Diverging Trend
Loan loss ratios and provision trends
Barclays' loan loss ratio has risen steadily from 7.4% to 7.8%, while several peers have seen improving credit trends — notably Wells Fargo (6.5% → 4.4%). Loan loss provisions as a share of net interest income are at 15.7%, placing Barclays in the upper end of the peer group. The business segment data points to the US Consumer Bank as the primary driver with a 41.3% loan loss ratio.
Loan Loss Ratio Trend
BankFY2023FY2024FY2025Direction
Barclays7.4%7.5%7.8%↑ Rising
Wells Fargo6.5%5.3%4.4%↓ Improving
BofA4.7%5.8%4.9%→ Stable
HSBC5.0%4.7%5.3%→ Stable
JPMorgan6.1%6.2%6.2%→ Stable
Citi10.1%12.1%11.0%↑ Elevated
Loan Losses / Net Interest Income (FY2025)
US Consumer Bank — Segment Detail
MetricFY2023FY2024FY2025
Operating Income Growth23.8%1.8%10.7%
EBT Margin5.3%12.2%14.0%
Cost / Income Ratio50.7%48.9%44.7%
Loan Loss Ratio44.0%38.9%41.3%
Barclays is absorbing a growing share of its net interest income into provisions at a time when several peers are seeing improving credit trends. The US consumer book appears to be the primary driver. This is worth understanding more deeply.
Confidential
Barclays PLC — Peer Benchmarking Analysis
Valuation
The Market's View: Valuation and Sentiment
Price multiples, analyst consensus, and shareholder returns
There is a visible tension in the data. Barclays delivered 48.4% total shareholder return over the past year — third best in the group — and 75% of analysts rate it a buy. Yet it trades at just 0.97x book value and 8.1x earnings, both significantly below the peer average. The market recognises the improvement but hasn't fully re-rated the franchise.
Price / Book Value (Feb 2026)
Price / Earnings (Feb 2026)
Analyst Consensus Recommendations
What would it take for the market to re-rate Barclays more in line with peers showing similar operational trajectories? The gap between operational improvement and market recognition is where the strategic conversation sits.
Confidential
Barclays PLC — Peer Benchmarking Analysis
Forward Look
Forward Outlook: What Analysts Expect
Consensus estimates 2026E — 2028E
Analyst consensus projects Barclays to deliver a 4.0% operating income CAGR through 2028 — broadly in line with the peer median. However, the expected EBT margin improvement of +3.5 percentage points is the highest in the peer group, indicating that analysts believe the efficiency story has further to run.
2026E–2028E Operating Income CAGR
2026E–2028E EBT Margin Change (ppts)
Forward Estimates — Barclays vs Peer Median
MetricBarclays 2026EPeer Median 2026EBarclays 2027EPeer Median 2027EBarclays 2028EPeer Median 2028E
OI YoY Growth6.3%6.5%4.3%4.0%3.8%4.4%
EBT Margin34.3%35.3%36.5%37.1%37.8%37.2%
The consensus view is that margin expansion has further to run. The question for this room is: what are the levers that make that happen, and where does the data suggest the biggest gaps still exist?
